page.title: NAFLD / MASH (Metabolic-Associated Steatohepatitis) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis: 104 alterations and 292 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: MASH (formerly NASH) with fibrosis progresses to cirrhosis in ~20% over 10 years on standard care (which until 2024 was lifestyle modification only); fibrosis regression occurs spontaneously in ~25% over 5 years with sustained weight loss
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Resmetirom (Rezdiffra) — first FDA-approved pharmacotherapy for MASH (March 2024): 26-30% histological resolution at 52 weeks vs 10% placebo; bariatric surgery achieves MASH resolution in 85% and fibrosis regression in 35% at 5 years; >7-10% weight loss resolves MASH in ~50%
remission.gap_size: Large — first drug only just approved in 2024 after decades of failed trials; practice still dominated by lifestyle advice alone
remission.barrier_detail: Until March 2024, there was literally no approved pharmacotherapy for MASH despite it being a leading cause of cirrhosis and liver transplant; the field repeatedly failed Phase 3 (obeticholic acid, elafibranor, selonsertib, cenicriviroc — all negative); resmetirom is a genuine breakthrough but access and prescribing uptake are still ramping up
remission.last_soc_change: March 2024 — resmetirom (Rezdiffra) FDA approved — the first pharmacotherapy for MASH; prior standard was weight loss and lifestyle only since MASH was described in 1980
remission.outdated_treatments: Vitamin E (early RCT positive in non-diabetic NASH — PIVENS trial; subsequent use became widespread but long-term safety concerns emerged; not guideline-recommended in diabetics or cirrhosis); metformin (repeatedly negative in MASH RCTs despite glucose benefit); omega-3 fatty acids at standard doses (Phase 3 negative for histology despite lipid benefit)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Coffee consumption (3+ cups/day associated with 40% reduced fibrosis progression — not a supplement, but a genuine finding); prebiotic/probiotic interventions; SGLT2 inhibitors (secondary benefit on liver histology in MASH — RCT data emerging); exercise as primary therapy (reduces liver fat independently of weight loss)
remission.frontier_signal: Resmetirom (just approved); semaglutide for MASH (Phase 3 ESSENCE trial — histological resolution 63% vs 34%; results 2024); lanifibranor (PPAR-alpha/delta/gamma, Phase 3 NATIVE trial positive); combination approaches; FGF21 analogs (pegbelfermin); FXR agonists next generation
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
